title: Roasted Indian Fish Fillet Curry
description: A flavorful and aromatic Indian-inspired dish featuring a crumbly roasted fish fillet curry. This recipe is perfect for a quick and energizing dinner on a weeknight.

ingredients.name: Curry powder
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought, whichever suits your taste.

ingredients.name: Garam masala
ingredients.notes: Available at most Indian grocery stores or online.

ingredients.name: Cumin
ingredients.notes: Use whole cumin seeds if you can.

ingredients.name: Coriander
ingredients.notes: Use whole coriander seeds if you can.

ingredients.name: Coconut oil
ingredients.notes: Unrefined or refined, whichever suits your taste.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince or crush for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Grate or mince for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Greek yogurt
ingredients.notes: Use non-fat or low-fat for a healthier option.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use sea salt or kosher salt for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed or bottled, whichever suits your taste.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Use as needed to adjust the curry's consistency.

instructions: Season the fish fillet with salt and set aside.
In a small pan, heat the coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the curry powder, garam masala, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and red chili powder. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes until the spices are fragrant.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger. Cook, stirring constantly, for another minute until the aromatics are fragrant.
Add the fish fillet to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until it's browned and cooked through.
Transfer the fish to a baking sheet and roast in the preheated oven for 8-10 minutes, until it's cooked through and flaky.
While the fish is roasting, prepare the curry sauce by mixing the Greek yogurt, lemon juice, and water in a small bowl.
Remove the fish from the oven and let it rest for 2-3 minutes before serving.
To assemble the dish, place the fish on a serving platter or individual plates. Spoon some of the curry sauce over the top of the fish, followed by a sprinkle of chopped cilantro, if desired.
Serve with naan or basmati rice, if desired.
